title: First-Time Traveler Airport Guide

sections.title: Arriving at the Airport
tips: Arrive at least 2.5–3 hours before international flights, or 1.5–2 hours for domestic.
Follow signage for your airline or terminal. When in doubt, ask airport staff.
Locate trolleys near the entrance if you have heavy luggage.
Keep your booking confirmation or e-ticket accessible on your phone.

sections.title: Check-In
tips: Find your airline's check-in counters. The screen displays show which desks are open.
Online check-in (via the airline's app or website) saves time – do it 24 hours before departure.
Hand over your passport (and visa if required) to the agent at the counter.
Confirm your seat assignment and ask for a window or aisle if you have a preference.
Collect your boarding pass – it shows your gate, seat number, and boarding time.

sections.title: Baggage Drop
tips: If you checked in online, use the dedicated 'Bag Drop' counters to save time.
Remove prohibited items (sharp objects, liquids over 100 ml) before reaching the counter.
Attach a luggage tag with your name and contact number in case it gets lost.
Keep your baggage receipt – you'll need it to claim your bag at the destination.
Never leave your bag unattended at the airport.

sections.title: Security Screening
tips: Place laptops, tablets, and large electronics in a separate tray.
Remove your belt, watch, and items from your pockets before going through the scanner.
Liquids must be in containers of 100 ml or less and placed in a clear, resealable bag (1 litre max).
Wear easy-to-remove shoes if possible.
Follow the security officer's instructions calmly; they are there to keep everyone safe.
Collect all your belongings from the trays before moving on.

sections.title: Passport Control
tips: For international travel, you will pass through passport control after security.
Have your passport open to the photo page and your boarding pass ready.
Many airports have e-Gates for eligible passport holders – follow the instructions on the screen.
Answer the officer's questions honestly and concisely.
Do not use your phone while standing in the queue or at the counter.

sections.title: Finding Your Gate
tips: Your gate number is printed on your boarding pass (e.g., B14).
Follow the overhead signs to your concourse and gate.
Gates can change – check the departure screens regularly.
Some airports have shuttle trains or buses between terminals; allow extra time.
Grab food or water before reaching the gate area if needed.

sections.title: Boarding
tips: Listen for boarding announcements. Most airlines board by zone or seat row.
Have your boarding pass (printed or on your phone) and passport ready to scan.
Store your carry-on bag in the overhead locker above your seat.
Place your personal item (backpack, handbag) under the seat in front of you.
Sit in your assigned seat – the crew may need to re-balance the aircraft.
Turn your phone to airplane mode once the doors close.

general_reminders: Stay hydrated – airports and planes are very dry environments.
Keep your documents (passport, boarding pass, visa) in one secure place at all times.
If you feel lost or anxious, airport information desks and airline staff are always happy to help.
Allow extra time for everything – rushing is the main cause of missed flights.
